VAL-INTEGRATION-001.title: OpenClaw manifest matches implemented API routes
VAL-INTEGRATION-001.notes: All 9 routes declared in openclaw-manifest.json are implemented in server.go with matching paths, methods, and auth expectations. Manifest declares: health (GET), auth/login (POST), courses (GET), lectures (GET), jobs (GET/POST), jobs/{id} (GET/DELETE), ws (GET). All routes verified via curl: public routes (health, login) return 200/401 as expected, protected routes return 401 without token.

VAL-INTEGRATION-002.title: API error-code contract matches runtime behavior

VAL-INTEGRATION-003.title: API endpoint response shapes are explicitly consistent
VAL-INTEGRATION-003.notes:
Response shapes verified:
- GET /health: Bare object {"status":"ok"} (not wrapped)
- POST /auth/login: Success envelope {success, data: {token, expires}}
- GET /courses: Bare array []client.Course (not wrapped)
- GET /lectures: Bare array []client.Lecture (not wrapped)
- GET /jobs: Bare array []Job (not wrapped)
- POST /jobs: Job object directly (201, not wrapped)
- DELETE /jobs/{id}: Success envelope {success, data: {id, status}}
This matches docs/api-reference.md which documents the mixed response formats explicitly.

VAL-INTEGRATION-004.title: CLI JSON automation contract matches runtime behavior

VAL-INTEGRATION-005.title: Local API health and auth bootstrap are machine-usable
VAL-INTEGRATION-005.notes:
Machine-usable bootstrap flow verified:
1. GET /api/v1/health returns {"status":"ok"} (200) - no auth required
2. POST /api/v1/auth/login with JSON body returns {success, data: {token, expires}} (200)
3. Token works for protected routes: GET /api/v1/courses with Bearer token returns course array (200)
Complete agent-ready flow: discover health -> authenticate -> access protected resources.

VAL-INTEGRATION-006.title: Request ID correlation works on representative API responses
VAL-INTEGRATION-006.notes:
X-Request-ID correlation verified on all responses:
- GET /health: Auto-generated X-Request-Id: 26b6f511-70c7-4744-989d-6da67198edf5
- GET /courses (protected): Auto-generated X-Request-Id in response headers
- GET /health with custom header: X-Request-Id: test-request-123 (propagated from request)
- GET /courses without auth (error): X-Request-Id in error response headers
CORS headers correctly expose X-Request-ID: Access-Control-Expose-Headers: X-Request-ID

summary: Tested 6 assertions: all 6 passed. All integration surfaces (API routes, error codes, response shapes, CLI JSON mode, auth bootstrap, request correlation) match documented contracts in docs/openclaw-manifest.json and docs/api-reference.md.
